```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))
## Parametros
theta <- sample(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 1)
n     <- sample(c(25, 36, 50, 100), 1)

## Para Exp(theta): I(theta) = 1/theta^2
## LICR = 1/(n * I(theta)) = theta^2/n
correct <- round(theta^2 / n, 4)
w1 <- round(1 / (n * theta^2), 4)
w2 <- round(theta^2 * n, 4)
w3 <- round(theta / n, 4)
w4 <- round(n / theta^2, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
O **Limite Inferior de Cramer-Rao** (LICR) para a variancia de qualquer estimador nao-viesado de $\theta$ e:
$$\text{LICR}(\theta) = \frac{1}{n \cdot I(\theta)},$$
onde $I(\theta)$ e a informacao de Fisher de uma unica observacao.

Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Exp}(\theta)$, tem-se $I(\theta) = 1/\theta^2$. Com $\theta = `r theta`$ e $n = `r n`$, qual e o LICR para estimadores nao-viesados de $\theta$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Com $I(\theta) = 1/\theta^2$:
$$\text{LICR}(\theta) = \frac{1}{n \cdot I(\theta)} = \frac{1}{n \cdot (1/\theta^2)} = \frac{\theta^2}{n}.$$

Para $\theta = `r theta`$ e $n = `r n`$:
$$\text{LICR} = \frac{`r theta`^2}{`r n`} = \frac{`r theta^2`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$

Nota: o estimador $\bar{X}$ e nao-viesado para $1/\theta$ neste modelo, e $1/\bar{X}$ e o EMV de $\theta$. O LICR indica a menor variancia possivel para qualquer estimador nao-viesado de $\theta$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap4S2Q5_LICR_Exponencial
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
